checks.a_analysis: The output text faithfully follows the source document's content. There are no invented paragraphs, sentences, or section headings. The preamble clauses, the resolution itself, and the signature details are all derived directly from the source.
The source document contains no explicit contact information like phone numbers or emails, and the output correctly reflects this without hallucinating any data.
All sections of the one-page resolution are present in the output, including the header, title, 'WHEREAS' clauses, the resolution statement, the date, the signature block, and the county seal.

The signature block has been structured as a table in the output. The source features two primary signer sections (Chairman and Clerk), and the output table correctly represents these in two distinct rows.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: All text within each section is fully captured. No sentences or significant portions of text are missing.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output accurately matches the text in the source. Punctuation, capitalization, and naming conventions (e.g., 'Norma J. Torres', '9-1-1 SAVES Act') are correctly preserved.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the entire output JSON and compared it against the source document. Each paragraph, heading, and clause from the original document (including the five 'WHEREAS' clauses, the 'NOW, THEREFORE' section, and the signature blocks) appears exactly once in the output. there is no evidence of repeated blocks of text.

The main document title is correctly identified as a heading. It is assigned level 2 (H2). While there is no H1 in the body of the document, the metadata contains a title, and starting with an H2 is a permissible adjustment for normalization. No levels are skipped, and the hierarchy correctly identifies the primary document title.

The signature block has been structured as a table. While the source document is not a formal table, using a table to organize the signatures and their respective titles/roles is an acceptable structural choice. The headers 'Field' and 'Value' are AI-generated for accessibility, and the data rows correspond to the signature blocks in the source.

The output JSON follows the logical top-to-bottom reading order of the source document: Header (North Carolina Rockingham County) -> Title -> Whereas clauses -> Resolution -> Date -> Signature Table -> County Seal. This sequence preserves the legal and logical flow of the original document.

The source document does not contain bulleted or numbered lists. The sequence of 'WHEREAS' clauses is appropriately rendered as separate paragraphs, which is faithful to the original structural design.
The source document contains no hyperlinks. No links were added to the output JSON.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the document matches the source PDF. The HTML layout translates the original's single-column text structure into a responsive web format. The header information, title, 'WHEREAS' clauses, and 'RESOLVED' section appear in the same relative order. The transformation of the signature lines into a structured table is a standard and acceptable layout change for accessibility, and the inclusion of the original page as an image at the bottom maintains visual fidelity. There are no multi-column collapses or rearrangements that confuse the meaning.

The 'Signature Section' table in the HTML output is a generated structure representing the signature blocks of the PDF. This table is correctly rendered with clear borders, distinct headers ('Field', 'Value'), and aligned content. Although the 'Value' column is empty (as signatures are non-textual images in the PDF), the table itself is not visually corrupted, overlapped, or misaligned. A user can easily read and understand the relationship between the fields and their intended purpose.

All meaningful visual elements from the source, including the Rockingham County seal and the handwritten signatures of Mark F. Richardson and Susan O. Washburn, are preserved and visible in the HTML output within the full-page image reproduction included at the end of the document. There are no broken image placeholders or missing meaningful graphics.

There is no duplication of body content in the rendered HTML. The text content is provided once as accessible HTML text. While the same information is visible in the image of the original document appended to the bottom, this is an intentional accessibility feature (providing both a machine-readable version and the original visual context) and does not constitute a content duplication defect as per the guidelines.
